senses.definition: to quickly turn a disadvantageous circumstance into a beneficial one

examples.x: The only question is whether the President can use his extraordinary political skills to turn the tables on his opponents.
The plaintiff‚Äôs lawyer turned the tables this morning by producing some strong new evidence.
With a sudden twist of events, the underdog team managed to turn the tables and secure a victory.
The clever negotiation tactic allowed the smaller company to turn the tables on their larger competitor during the contract talks.
She was initially behind in the competition, but her determination and hard work helped her turn the tables and win the championship.

senses.definition: to change an unfavorable situation so that it works to one's advantage

examples.x: He claimed that his government was turning the tide in the war against drugs.
The votes in the swing state may turn the tide in this election.
Wow, they really turned the tide on their opponents by scoring 20 unanswered points!
After a series of losses, the team's star player made a game-changing play that turned the tide in their favor.
Public awareness campaigns on climate change aim to turn the tide against environmental degradation.

word_list.pronunciation: lňą√¶f …Ďňźn√į…™ ňą Ć√į…ö sňąa…™d  Ćv wňą Ćnz fňąe…™s

senses.definition: to say that someone's happiness or success will not last long as the situation is about to change

examples.x: The league leaders will be laughing on the other side of their faces if they lose tomorrow.
He may be laughing on the other side of his face once he realizes the challenges that lie ahead in the project.
She laughed on the other side of her face when her seemingly lucrative investment turned into a loss.
Don't be too quick to gloat; you might end up laughing on the other side of your face if things don't go as planned.
His confident predictions about the election had him laughing on the other side of his face when the results were announced.

word_list.word: the shoe is on the other foot

senses.definition: used to refer to a situation that has completely changed in one's favor

examples.x: I remember how you used to patronize me, but the shoe is on the other foot now.
He used to be the boss's favorite, but now that he's made a mistake, the shoe is on the other foot, and he's facing consequences.
In the rivalry between the two teams, this season, the shoe is on the other foot, with the underdog team leading the competition.
After years of dominating the market, the corporation is now facing stiff competition, and the shoe is on the other foot as they struggle to maintain their position.
She used to be the one giving advice, but now, with her own challenges, the shoe is on the other foot, and she's seeking guidance.

senses.definition: used to refer to a sudden change in a situation where one or a group of people who were weak, unsuccessful, oppressed, etc. become strong, successful, liberated, etc.

examples.x: After years of being mistreated by their employer, the employees finally had enough, and the worm turned as they went on strike.
She endured years of bullying, but one day, the worm turned, and she stood up to her tormentors.
When the previously submissive character in the story decided to take a stand, it was a moment when the worm turned.
In the face of constant criticism, he reached a breaking point, and the worm turned as he defended his work and his worth.
The nation had been subject to a dictatorship for decades, but eventually, the worm turned, and the people rose up to demand freedom and democracy.

senses.definition: the contrasting experiences of both prosperity and adversity that individuals may encounter in life

examples.x: In their marriage, they promised to stand by each other through weal and woe, supporting one another in all circumstances.
The novel explores the characters' journeys through weal and woe, showcasing the complexities of human existence.
Life is a journey filled with weal and woe, and it's essential to appreciate the lessons that both prosperity and adversity bring.
The friends had shared many moments of weal and woe, celebrating each other's successes and offering comfort in times of hardship.
The nation's history is marked by its people's resilience through weal and woe, as they faced both triumphs and challenges.

senses.definition: used to say that a person's opinion or a situation has changed

examples.x: The team was on a losing streak, but then the tide turned and they went on to win the championship.
